input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-13T01:01:49.014Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

### XAML ‚Äî use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome!"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page respects your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device theme at startup **and** reacts to changes at runtime ‚Äî no event wiring needed.

### C# equivalent ‚Äî `SetAppTheme` extension method

```csharp
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome!", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));
//                 BindableProperty          Light value   Dark value
```

`SetAppTheme(property, lightValue, darkValue)` does the same thing as the XAML markup extension.

### Key points

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Follows `Application.RequestedTheme` (maps to OS setting) |
| **Live updates** | Yes ‚Äî switches automatically when the user toggles system theme |
| **Override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme regardless of OS |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For larger apps, define colors/styles in `AppTheme`-keyed resources instead of inline values |

### Scaling up with resource dictionaries

Instead of repeating hex codes, define themed colors once in `App.xaml`:

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>
    <!-- won't work directly ‚Äî use OnPlatform/AppThemeBinding in Style setters -->

    <Style TargetType="Label">
        <Setter Property="TextColor"
                Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />
    </Style>
</Application.Resources>
```

Then pages automatically inherit the style without per-element bindings.
